headline_drivers: Q1 2026 results: revenue ~$7.1B, +46–47% Y/Y and ~10%+ above ~$6.4B consensus; net income $438M and adj. EBITDA ~$1.0B, showing strong operating leverage despite an EPS miss vs consensus (reported May 12, 2026)

Record Shopee GMV and broad-based strength across Shopee, Monee (fintech), and Garena, reinforcing the view that Sea’s growth engines have re-accelerated after a period of margin-focused retrenchment (commentary across earnings coverage May 12–13, 2026)

Analyst and market reaction framing the quarter as a ‘growth is back’ print: Street still largely at Moderate Buy with targets well above the tape, plus heavy call-option activity and inclusion on momentum/volume-spike screens, helping fuel a squeeze/positioning reset (reports and options-flow notes May 12–13, 2026)

Context from prior quarters: very strong FY 2025 / Q4 2025 revenue growth (36–38%) and net income tripling to ~$1.6B, yet the stock sold off hard on margin/future-EPS worries and even hit 52‑week lows; the Q1 2026 print is being read as validating the longer‑term growth/profitability story and unwinding some of that pessimism (Q4 2025 results and subsequent downgrades in March 2026, plus 52‑week low reports thereafter)
summary: The move is being driven by Sea Limited’s Q1 2026 earnings, where revenue (~$7.1B, +46–47% Y/Y) and profitability (net income $438M, adj. EBITDA ~$1.0B) significantly outpaced expectations even though EPS missed, and Shopee posted record GMV with strength also in Monee and Garena. Coming after a period when the stock was punished to 52‑week lows despite very strong 2025 growth, the print is seen as confirming a renewed growth-and-profitability trajectory; analysts remain broadly positive, options activity has turned unusually bullish, and that combination of fundamental upside, positioning reset, and momentum screens explains the 1‑day +15% move on ~4x average volume.
